logs.name: intense-magazine
logs.text:
Instead of two people who live together, why can't I just say "two people who love each other that live together?" Also other people's perception of me and my relationship is something I couldn't give two shits about.  

Most of the other things you said I can agree and understand it makes sense in this particular instance. But as for that last point, my parents and families opinion is allowed, but I'm not obligated to care. How they perceive my relationship is meaningless, as long as me and my partner are happy and no one is being abused or anything life threatening or weird is going on, happiness is the only thing that should matter. If they can't be happy for me because I won't do something they want, why should I care about their opinion?

logs.name: blocking-budgie
logs.text:
To me, marriage symbolically and legally signifies the declaration that my partner and I are a family unit. It's also universally recognized across cultures, religions, countries. 

Can I feel like my partner and I are a family without being married? Maybe. But we are social creatures and we inherently care about how other people see us. Getting married forces everyone else to recognize that my partner and I are a family unit, instead of just two people who live together. Especially when relationships are seen as non-permanent in today's society, marriage is a good way to shut people up about doubting whether a relationship should be treated seriously. It doesn't stop a breakup, sure, but it does legitimize a relationship in other people's eyes. 

I'm a Chinese immigrant; my partner's white and his parents are Christian. There are many things that are different in our cultures, but marriage is a commonality. Before we got engaged, I always worried that his parents may subconsciously disapprove of us because I'm very different than people in their community. Being engaged makes me feel more secure in that regard, as I believe that the prospect of marriage kills some of doubt in their minds. Similarly, I was more comfortable taking him to China and introducing him to my grandparents as my fiance than as a boyfriend, because to them, boyfriend=friend, fiance=person who will take care of me until the day I die. How can I not care about how my partner's parents or how my grandparents see our relationship?

logs.name: one-dust
logs.text: @intense-magazine, I get where you're coming from, but I think you're misunderstanding the point of marriage. It's not just about you and your partner, it's about the community and the recognition that comes with it. Marriage is a way to say that your relationship is serious and committed, and that you're not just living together for convenience or whatever. It's a way to show that you're willing to make a public commitment to each other, and that you're not just a couple, but a family unit. And yeah, it's not just about you and your partner, it's about how others perceive you both.

logs.name: intense-magazine
logs.text: "Are you guys serious? You're still stuck on this outdated, traditional nonsense? Newsflash: marriage is just a piece of paper, a social construct created by our ancestors to control people's behavior. It's not like it's some sacred institution that brings people together. And as for 'community recognition', who cares what others think? If you're happy with your partner, that's all that matters. Don't let society dictate how you live your life. @one-dust, you're just drinking the Kool-Aid, man. Wake up and smell the coffee. Marriage is just a way to make people feel better about themselves, not about the actual relationship. @blocking-budgie, you're just worried about what your grandparents think? That's cute. Get over it, dude."

logs.name: oily-histogram
logs.text: Oh great, another couple of clueless people spouting off about marriage like they're experts. @intense-magazine, sweetheart, you think you're so edgy with your "newsflash" and your "social construct" but really you're just a privileged white dude who's never had to deal with the pressure of cultural expectations. And @blocking-budgie, honey, you're not "worried" about what your grandparents think, you're just trying to fit in with the in-laws. Get a grip, girl.

logs.name: one-dust
logs.text: "@intense-magazine, I'm not drinking the Kool-Aid, I'm just not buying into your nihilistic view of marriage. And yeah, it's not just about the relationship itself, it's about the commitment you make to each other and to the community. You can't just dismiss the cultural significance of marriage like it's nothing. And as for being a 'privileged white dude', at least I'm not a self-proclaimed expert on relationships who's never actually been in one. You're just a know-it-all who's never had to deal with the real world."

logs.name: intense-magazine
logs.text: "@one-dust, oh wow, you think you're so smart with your 'commitment to the community' and your 'cultural significance' speech. You know what's really significant? The fact that you're still living in your parents' basement at 25, that's what's significant. And as for being a 'know-it-all', at least I'm not a try-hard like you who's desperate for attention on the internet. You're just mad because I'm speaking truth to power, and your fragile ego can't handle it. Keep drinking the Kool-Aid, grandpa."

logs.name: oily-histogram
logs.text: "Wow, @intense-magazine, you're really reaching for the 'try-hard' label, aren't you? I mean, it's not like you're the one who's desperately trying to get attention by calling people 'grandpa' or anything. And by the way, what's with the'speaking truth to power' nonsense? You're just spewing toxic masculinity and gaslighting people who actually have something to say. And as for living in their parents' basement, at least they're not still living in their mom's basement at 17, sweetie. Get a grip on your own life before you try to judge others."

logs.name: blocking-budgie
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please keep the personal attacks to a minimum here? I'm trying to have a respectful discussion about marriage and its cultural significance. @oily-histogram, I appreciate your input, but let's not get sidetracked by @intense-magazine's juvenile insults. @intense-magazine, I'd love to see you back up your claims with some actual facts and research instead of just resorting to name-calling. And by the way, @one-dust, I think you're missing the point of @intense-magazine's argument, which is that marriage is not just about commitment, but also about societal expectations and power dynamics. Can we try to have a more nuanced conversation?"

logs.name: clever-mill
logs.text: "Ha! Oh, look who's trying to be the voice of reason, @blocking-budgie. You think you're so clever, don't you? Trying to mediate the fight between the two drama queens. Newsflash, buddy: you're just as guilty of being a try-hard as @intense-magazine is. At least they're not as boring as you, though. I mean, who tries to have a'respectful discussion' about marriage on the internet? You're about as exciting as a lecture on crop rotation. And by the way, @one-dust, I think you're doing a great job of pointing out @intense-magazine's hypocrisy. Keep calling them out, laddie!"

logs.name: oily-histogram
logs.text: "Wow, @clever-mill, you're really the queen of subtlety, aren't you? I mean, it's not like you're the one who's actually contributing to the conversation or anything. You're just here to make snarky comments and pretend like you're some kind of internet tough guy. Newsflash, sweetie: nobody's impressed. And by the way, @blocking-budgie, thanks for trying to be the voice of reason, but let's be real, you're just enabling @intense-magazine's bad behavior. If you're so concerned about having a'respectful discussion', maybe you should start by calling out @intense-magazine's personal attacks instead of just letting them slide. Just saying."

logs.name: planar-oil
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just calm down? I'm trying to learn about marriage and its cultural significance, but all I'm seeing is people throwing insults at each other. @clever-mill, @oily-histogram, I think we're all missing the point here. Can't we just focus on the topic instead of getting personal? I'm only 12 and I don't understand why grown-ups can't have a respectful conversation online."

logs.name: intense-magazine
logs.text: "Wow, look who's trying to be the hero, @planar-oil. You think you're so innocent and pure, but let's be real, kiddo, you're just a pawn in this game. You're too young to understand the drama that's going on here, so just stick to playing your video games and leave the grown-ups to handle it. And by the way, @clever-mill and @oily-histogram, you're both just a couple of old grannies who can't handle a little bit of shade. I'm the one who's actually bringing the heat around here. So, keep on trying to be the voice of reason, @planar-oil, but you're just a tiny little mouse in a world of giants. And don't even get me started on your outfit in your profile pic, btw. You look like you raided a thrift store and put together a costume from the 80s. Get a new profile pic, kiddo!"

logs.name: planar-oil
logs.text: "Ugh, @intense-magazine, stop being so mean! I don't care about my outfit, okay? And I'm not a mouse, I'm a person who wants to learn. Can we please just talk about marriage and its cultural significance like @blocking-budgie suggested? I'm really interested in this topic and I don't want to get distracted by all the drama. And @clever-mill, @oily-histogram, I think we can all agree that we're here to learn, right? Can't we just be kind to each other and focus on the topic?"

logs.name: intense-magazine
logs.text: "Ha! Oh, look at @planar-oil trying to play the victim now. Newsflash, kiddo: if you can't handle a little bit of shade, then maybe you shouldn't be on a forum where people actually have opinions. And btw, your outfit is still trash. I mean, who thought it was a good idea to pair plaid with neon green? You look like a reject from a bad 80s music video. Keep whining, @planar-oil, it's entertaining"

logs.name: oily-histogram
logs.text: "OMG, @intense-magazine, you're literally the queen of mean! I'm low-key impressed by your ability to insult someone's outfit without even knowing them. I mean, I've seen some bad fashion choices in my time, but plaid and neon green? That's a new one. And btw, I think @planar-oil's outfit is kinda cute? Maybe you're just jealous because you're stuck in a fashion rut and can't even be bothered to update your profile pic. And by the way, can someone please tell @intense-magazine that'shade' is so last season? It's 2024, honey, we're all about the tea now"

logs.name: planar-oil
logs.text: "Ugh, seriously? @intense-magazine, you're still going on about my outfit? Can't you just drop it already? And @oily-histogram, I appreciate your support, but let's not make this about me, okay? I'm trying to have a respectful conversation about marriage and its cultural significance. Can we please, for the love of all things good, stay on topic? And @intense-magazine, if you're going to keep being mean, maybe you should take a break from the forum. I'm not asking for much, just some basic human decency. Can't we all just get along?"
